title: pisum commune subsp. sativum
intro: Pisum commune subsp. sativum, also known as garden pea, is a subspecies of the Pisum commune plant, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a cool-season legume that has been cultivated for its edible seeds and pods. The medicinal properties of Pisum commune subsp. sativum include its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and supplements, offering potential health benefits such as improved digestion, immune system support, and reduced risk of chronic diseases.
